protocols.name: AIP
protocols.description: The Autoimmune Protocol (AIP) is an elimination diet designed to help identify food sensitivities and reduce inflammation in people with autoimmune conditions.

phases.name: Reintroduction
phases.description: Systematically reintroduce foods one at a time

protocols.name: Keto
protocols.description: A ketogenic diet is high in fats, moderate in protein, and very low in carbohydrates to induce ketosis.

protocols.name: Low FODMAP
protocols.description: The Low FODMAP diet restricts fermentable carbohydrates that can trigger IBS symptoms, developed by Monash University.

protocols.name: Low Histamine
protocols.description: A low histamine diet eliminates foods high in histamine or that trigger histamine release, helping manage histamine intolerance symptoms.

phases.name: Reintroduction
phases.description: Test medium histamine foods one at a time

protocols.name: Low Lectin
protocols.description: A low lectin diet eliminates foods high in lectins, which some believe can cause inflammation and digestive issues.

protocols.name: Low Oxalate
protocols.description: A low oxalate diet restricts foods high in oxalates to prevent kidney stones and manage oxalate sensitivity.

protocols.name: No Nightshades
protocols.description: A nightshade-free diet eliminates vegetables from the nightshade family, which can trigger inflammation in sensitive individuals.

protocols.name: Paleo
protocols.description: The Paleo diet emphasizes whole foods that were available to Paleolithic humans, excluding processed foods, grains, and legumes.

phases.name: Modified
phases.description: Allow some flexibility with certain foods

protocols.name: Whole30
protocols.description: Whole30 is a 30-day elimination diet that removes sugar, alcohol, grains, legumes, and dairy to reset eating habits.

responseData.error: Failed to complete get journal entries
